fields.description:
## Dependencies (STACKED — review with --ref HEAD)
Builds on **ddbe** (identity + mappings) and **264f** (`resolve_mapping`), committed
on this branch, not yet on origin/main. Review with `--ref HEAD`.

## Context
An inbound external user (e.g. a Jira assignee/reporter with an `accountId` +
`displayName`) that has NO local identity mapping mints a PLACEHOLDER identity
storing the raw `(provider, external_id, display_name)`. Re-running resolution after
a real mapping appears UPGRADES the placeholder in place — idempotently, one identity
not two — keyed on the provider's stable opaque id (Gitea's OriginalAuthorID
discipline).

## Why
When rebar pulls a Jira ticket, its assignee/reporter/comment authors are external
Jira users we may not yet know. Minting a durable placeholder (keyed on the stable
accountId) preserves attribution without guessing an email, and lets a later real
mapping enrich the SAME identity rather than forking a duplicate.

## What
### 1. Resolve-or-mint seam (`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py`)
`ensure_identity_for(provider, external_id, display_name, *, repo_root=None) -> str`:
- IDEMPOTENT resolve-first: call `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id, repo_root=...)`
  (264f). If it returns an existing identity id, RETURN it (do NOT create a second) —
  this is what makes a re-run yield ONE identity. Optionally UPGRADE: if that identity
  is still a placeholder and `display_name` is non-empty and differs from its current
  title, update the title in place (`edit_ticket`) — the upgrade path.
- Else MINT a placeholder: `create_identity(name=display_name or external_id,
  email="", mappings=[{"provider":provider, "external_id":external_id}], ...)` marked
  as a placeholder via a `placeholder` tag set ATOMICALLY at mint: extend
  `create_identity_core` / `create_identity` with an optional `tags` list that is
  forwarded to `create_core(..., tags=...)` (create_core already accepts `tags`), so
  the CREATE event carries `tags=["placeholder"]` in one event (no separate tag() call,
  no tagless window). `is_placeholder` checks the `placeholder` tag in compiled state
  (the reducer surfaces CREATE tags). Return the new id.
- Never raises for a lookup problem (a resolve failure falls through to mint).
- `is_placeholder(identity_id, *, repo_root=None) -> bool` helper (the `placeholder`
  tag is present).
- The mapping key is ALWAYS the provider's opaque `external_id` (never email), so the
  resolve/upgrade is provider-neutral (works for jira/github/gitea alike).

### 2. Placeholder → real upgrade
When a real mapping/identity is later established for the same `(provider,
external_id)` — either a subsequent `ensure_identity_for` with a better
`display_name`, or an operator enriching the identity (email/keys) and removing the
`placeholder` tag — the SAME identity id is reused (resolve-first keys on
`external_id`). A test adds a real name/email to the placeholder, re-runs
`ensure_identity_for(same provider/external_id, ...)`, and asserts exactly ONE
identity exists for that mapping (not two) and it is the upgraded record.

### 3. Inbound reconciler wiring (`apply_inbound_records.py`)
Where the inbound apply reads the inbound Jira ASSIGNEE (the only inbound user field
today — `_extract_name(fields["assignee"])` at ~lines 113/258, which pulls the
`displayName`), ALSO resolve the inbound user through `ensure_identity_for("jira",
<accountId>, <displayName>)` so an unmapped inbound user mints/reuses a placeholder
identity and the local attribution references a stable identity. Keep the existing
name extraction as the human-readable fallback; the identity minting is additive and
best-effort (a missing accountId falls back to the name-only behavior without
failing the inbound apply).

## Scope
In: `ensure_identity_for` + `is_placeholder` (idempotent resolve-or-mint, keyed on
external_id, placeholder tag, in-place upgrade) and the inbound-apply wiring. Out:
outbound mapping (264f), enforcement (3183). Concurrency: minting is idempotent for
SEQUENTIAL re-runs (resolve-first); the reconciler applies inbound records single-
writer, so no concurrent double-mint of the same accountId is expected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ensure_identity_for("jira", <accountId>, <displayName>)` on an accountId with
  no existing mapping creates a placeholder identity whose title is the displayName,
  whose `mappings` contains `{"provider":"jira","external_id":<accountId>}`, and which
  `is_placeholder` reports True (test).
- [ ] Re-running `ensure_identity_for` with the SAME `(provider, external_id)` returns
  the SAME identity id and does NOT create a second — a test asserts exactly ONE
  identity maps that accountId (list/search count == 1) after two calls, even when the
  second call supplies a different display_name (which upgrades the title in place).
- [ ] The mapping key is the provider's opaque `external_id` (not email), so an
  upgrade for a different provider/id is independent — a test with two different
  `external_id`s yields two distinct identities, and the same `external_id` under a
  different `display_name` still yields one.

- [ ] Inbound wiring (best-effort): given an inbound Jira assignee field with an
  `accountId` + `displayName` and no existing mapping, applying the inbound record
  mints/reuses a placeholder identity for that accountId (test drives the inbound
  apply path with a fixture record and asserts a placeholder identity exists for the
  accountId); a record missing an `accountId` falls back to name-only behavior WITHOUT
  failing the inbound apply.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (ensure_identity_for + is_placeholder)
- `src/rebar/__init__.py` + `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` (export ensure_identity_for/is_placeholder)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound_records.py` (resolve inbound Jira user via ensure_identity_for, additive/best-effort)
